req.title: Hr-Admin updates timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a timesheet through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates that an authenticated hr-admin can update timesheets via REST API with proper validation, audit trails, and error handling. The suite covers the happy path (successful update with audit record), mandatory field validation failures, authentication/authorization enforcement, and boundary conditions on field values. Tests are designed using equivalence partitioning (field validity classes), boundary value analysis (field length/range limits), negative path testing (missing auth, forbidden role), and decision table (validation rule combinations).

tests.name: test_hr_admin_updates_timesheet_success
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated with valid JWT token
timesheet with ID 'ts-001' exists in system
actions: Include valid Authorization header with Bearer token
expected: Response body contains timesheet entity with updated fields
Audit record written with action 'UPDATE', actor 'hr-admin', timestamp, and changes
trace: R-WEB-018: Expected outcome - timesheet is updated
R-WEB-018: Expected outcome - audit record written
R-WEB-018: Expected outcome - response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_hr_admin_updates_timesheet_success(base_url, hr_admin_token, audit_log_helper):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    payload = {
        "hours": 40,
        "date": "2024-01-15",
        "project_id": "proj-123",
        "description": "Development work"
    }
    resp = <requests_patch>(f"{base_url}/api/timesheets/ts-001", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    assert resp.json()["id"] == "ts-001"
    assert resp.json()["hours"] == 40
    audit = <audit_log_helper>(entity_id="ts-001", action="UPDATE")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["actor"] == "hr-admin"

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated with valid JWT token
hours field is defined as mandatory in schema
expected: Response body contains validation error message referencing 'hours' field
trace: R-WEB-018: Error pathway - 400 on validation error
R-WEB-018: Mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_unauthenticated
preconditions: no valid authentication token is provided
actions: Omit Authorization header or provide invalid token
expected: Response body contains authentication error message

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_insufficient_role
preconditions: user is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Include valid Authorization header with employee-role token
expected: Response body contains role/permission error message
trace: R-WEB-018: Error pathway - 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_boundary_hours_maximum
preconditions: hours field has maximum allowed value of 24
expected: Timesheet is updated with hours value 24
trace: R-WEB-018: Boundary value analysis on hours field (max value)

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_boundary_hours_exceeds_maximum
preconditions: hours field has maximum allowed value of 24
expected: Response body contains validation error for hours field
trace: R-WEB-018: Boundary value analysis on hours field (exceeds max)

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_boundary_description_length
preconditions: description field has maximum length of 500 characters
actions: Payload with description at maximum boundary: {"hours": 8, "description": "<500_char_string>"}
expected: Timesheet is updated with full description text
trace: R-WEB-018: Boundary value analysis on description field (max length)
